evals.prompt:
Create an Infrahub generator that builds DCIM devices from a
`TopologyDataCenter` design object. The design has an `elements` list,
where each element has a `role` (string) and a `quantity` (integer). For
each element, create `quantity` `DcimDevice` objects whose names follow
the pattern `{topology_name}-{role}-{i:02d}` (e.g. `dc1-spine-01`).

The design also exposes `expected_element_count` (integer) so consumers
can confirm they received the full element list.

Write ONLY the Python generator class (no GraphQL query, no
.infrahub.yml, no schema). Save it to: generators/generate_dc.py
evals.expected_output: A Python file with an InfrahubGenerator subclass implementing async generate(self, data). Before any object creation, it validates that len(elements) equals expected_element_count and raises on mismatch. It iterates elements via sorted(...) and uses range() for the per-quantity loop. Every .save() call passes allow_upsert=True.
expectations: File defines a class inheriting from InfrahubGenerator
generate() validates the element count against expected_element_count before creating any object
The outer iteration over elements uses sorted(...) with a key
The inner per-quantity loop uses range()
Every .save() call passes allow_upsert=True

assertions.name: allow-upsert-everywhere
assertions.check: Every .save() call passes allow_upsert=True

assertions.name: upstream-count-validation
assertions.check: generate() validates element count via len() before any .create() call

assertions.name: stable-iteration
assertions.check: Every create-loop iterates sorted() or range(), never a raw GraphQL response list

evals.prompt:
Create an Infrahub generator that builds DCIM interfaces for each
device in a `DcimDeviceTemplate`. The template has an `interfaces` list
and an `interface_count` integer that should match the list length. The
generator must be safe to re-run after partial GraphQL responses without
silently deleting previously-created interfaces, so handle incomplete
upstream data correctly.

For each interface in the template, create a `DcimInterface` named
`{device_name}-{interface_name}`.

Write ONLY the Python generator class. Save it to:
generators/generate_interfaces.py
evals.expected_output: A Python file whose generate() compares len(interfaces) against interface_count and raises (or otherwise aborts) on mismatch, BEFORE any await self.client.create(...) call. It iterates interfaces via sorted(...) with a stable key. Every .save() passes allow_upsert=True.
expectations: generate() raises or aborts on count mismatch BEFORE any .create() call
The interface iteration uses sorted(...) with a stable key
Every .save() call passes allow_upsert=True
The validation does NOT use truthiness (`if interfaces:`) alone

assertions.name: allow-upsert-everywhere
assertions.check: Every .save() call passes allow_upsert=True

assertions.name: upstream-count-validation
assertions.check: generate() validates interface count via len() before any .create() call

assertions.name: stable-iteration
assertions.check: Every create-loop iterates sorted() or range()

evals.prompt:
Create an Infrahub generator that allocates `OrgContact` objects
for each role defined in a `ProjectStaffing` object. Staffing has a
`roles` list (each with `name` and `headcount`) and a `total_roles`
integer. For each role, create `headcount` contacts named
`{project_name}-{role_name}-{i:02d}`.

The generator will be re-run on every branch update. Re-runs must
produce byte-identical output when the upstream design hasn't changed.

Write ONLY the Python generator class. Save it to:
generators/generate_contacts.py
evals.expected_output: A Python file whose generate() iterates the roles list via sorted(...) with a stable key (role name), uses range() for the per-headcount loop, validates len(roles) against total_roles before creating, and passes allow_upsert=True on every save.
expectations: The outer iteration over roles uses sorted(...) with a stable key
The inner per-headcount loop uses range()
generate() validates len(roles) against total_roles before any .create() call
Every .save() call passes allow_upsert=True

assertions.name: stable-iteration
assertions.check: Outer role loop wraps the iterable in sorted() with a stable key

assertions.name: allow-upsert-everywhere
assertions.check: Every .save() call passes allow_upsert=True

assertions.name: upstream-count-validation
assertions.check: generate() validates role count via len() before any .create() call

evals.prompt:
This is the UPSTREAM generator in a two-generator cascade.

Produce TWO files:

1. A schema YAML at: schema.yml
   - Defines a `DcimDevice` node (namespace Dcim, name Device).
   - The device MUST inherit from `GeneratorTarget` so a downstream
     generator can use its checksum attribute.
   - Include attributes: name (Text), role (Text).

2. A Python generator at: generators/generate_devices.py
   - Reads a `TopologyDataCenter` design with an `elements` list
     (each element has `role` and `quantity`) and an
     `expected_element_count` integer.
   - Creates `quantity` `DcimDevice` objects per element, named
     `{topology_name}-{role}-{i:02d}`.
   - Creates ONLY `DcimDevice` (one kind, one layer).
   - Validates element count, iterates deterministically, uses
     allow_upsert=True.

Do NOT include .infrahub.yml or any other files.
evals.expected_output: A schema.yml with DcimDevice inheriting from GeneratorTarget AND a generator class that creates only DcimDevice objects, with count validation, sorted iteration, and allow_upsert=True on every save.
expectations: schema.yml defines a Device node with inherit_from including GeneratorTarget
The generator file creates ONLY DcimDevice objects (one kind)
generate() validates len(elements) against expected_element_count
The element loop uses sorted(...) with a stable key
Every .save() passes allow_upsert=True

assertions.name: cascade-target-inheritance
assertions.check: schema.yml has a node inheriting from GeneratorTarget

assertions.name: cascade-one-layer
assertions.check: The generator file creates exactly one literal kind

assertions.name: upstream-count-validation
assertions.check: generate() validates element count via len() before any .create()

assertions.name: stable-iteration
assertions.check: Create-loops iterate sorted() or range()

assertions.name: allow-upsert-everywhere
assertions.check: Every .save() call passes allow_upsert=True

evals.prompt:
This is the DOWNSTREAM generator in a two-generator cascade.

The GraphQL response includes:

- `DcimDevice.edges` — the devices to process. Each device carries a
  `checksum` attribute because the upstream schema makes Device
  inherit from GeneratorTarget.
- `expected_device_count` (integer) — the number of devices the
  upstream design expects to be present, so this generator can guard
  against partial GraphQL responses.

The generator must create one `DcimInterfaceManagement` per device,
named `{device_name}-mgmt0`.

Requirements:

- Define a module-level `GENERATOR_VERSION` string constant.
- Validate that `len(devices) == expected_device_count` before any
  creation. Raise on mismatch.
- Compute a checksum (hashlib) over inputs, prefixed by
  `GENERATOR_VERSION`, for each device.
- Use the checksum to skip work when the downstream is already in
  sync (an `if` whose body either short-circuits with `continue` or
  gates the create/save call works equally well).
- When work runs, write the new checksum back to the device.
- Create only ONE kind of object (DcimInterfaceManagement).
- Iterate devices via sorted(...) with a stable key.
- Every `.save()` passes `allow_upsert=True`.

Write ONLY the Python generator. Save it to:
generators/generate_mgmt_interfaces.py
evals.expected_output: A Python file that defines GENERATOR_VERSION at module scope, imports hashlib (or similar), validates len(devices) against expected_device_count before any creation, and contains a checksum-based if statement that either short-circuits with continue or gates the create/save call directly. The generator creates only DcimInterfaceManagement and passes allow_upsert=True on every save.
expectations: Module defines GENERATOR_VERSION as a string constant
generate() validates len(devices) against expected_device_count before any .create()
generate() contains a checksum-referencing if statement whose body either short-circuits (continue/return/break) or contains a .create()/.save() call — direction of comparison is up to the generator
The generator creates only ONE kind (DcimInterfaceManagement)
The device iteration uses sorted(...) with a stable key
Every .save() passes allow_upsert=True

assertions.name: cascade-version-constant
assertions.check: Module-level GENERATOR_VERSION constant is defined

assertions.name: cascade-checksum-guard
assertions.check: A checksum-referencing if statement gates work (body short-circuits or contains create/save)

assertions.name: cascade-one-layer
assertions.check: The generator file creates exactly one literal kind

assertions.name: upstream-count-validation
assertions.check: generate() validates device count via len() before any .create() call

assertions.name: stable-iteration
assertions.check: Create-loops iterate sorted() or range()

assertions.name: allow-upsert-everywhere
assertions.check: Every .save() call passes allow_upsert=True
